moves.note: Slight trim, broad index stays present

plan: Sell both leveraged ETFs immediately — SOXL and SPAL are both deep in the red and daily decay compounds losses on margin; freeing that ~8% of book plus SMH trim proceeds funds the cash buffer.
Trim SMH from 13% to 7% to bring semiconductors sector below 15% of book, reducing the biggest concentration risk flagged in your data.
Increase QQQI to 23% and hold SPY at 35% — together these two income/broad-index positions form a 58% defensive core that dampens portfolio swings significantly.
Trim QQQ slightly to 12% since SPY already covers broad index exposure; combined broad index stays near 47%, just under the 45% single-sector cap when counted separately.
Raise cash buffer to roughly 18-20% from trimmed and sold proceeds — this directly reduces your true leverage below 1.0x and cuts the 2-sigma bad-month figure well below the current -17.91%.
PANW is a conditional Watch only at scanner entry 351.34-351.69 with stop 350.62 — only open if cash buffer is fully established first and position stays at 3% maximum.
conservative.read: This posture targets modest positive return in the range of 1-2% per month in calm markets, mainly from QQQI income and broad-index drift, with CRWD retained as the sole high-growth name. The tradeoff is real: you sacrifice the explosive upside of SOXL and chip concentration in exchange for a dramatically smaller bad-month scenario, but you still carry margin and tech risk, so a broad tech selloff will still hurt.

plan: Sell SPAL entirely — at 2% of book it moves the needle least but carries 2x decay and is already down -13.72%; proceeds go toward higher-quality adds.
Trim SOXL to 3% rather than zero — this keeps a small leveraged chip kicker if semis recover, but limits the drag and caps leveraged exposure well under the 5% balanced ceiling.
Trim SMH from 13% to 9% — semiconductors sector drops from 19% to roughly 12% of book, meaningfully reducing concentration while keeping direct chip exposure.
Add QQQI to 20% — the income layer moderates month-to-month swings and partially offsets margin cost, fitting the risk-first growth posture.
Open PANW at the scanner entry zone (351.34-351.69) with a hard stop at 350.62 as instructed — the 9/12 ACCUMULATE score and R of 6.01 make it the strongest setup in your data.
TSM is a Watch at 443.34-443.79 stop 442.47 — adds semiconductor quality without triple-leverage decay; only enter if the entry triggers and total book leverage stays under 1.1x after the add.
balanced.read: This balanced reshaping targets something in the 2-3% monthly range in favorable tape conditions, with the PANW add and retained CRWD providing growth while the income layer and broad indexes dampen swings. Your bad-month scenario improves modestly from the current -17.91% but you still run margin and meaningful tech concentration, so a sharp chip or broad-tech sell-off remains the key risk to watch.

moves.note: Lean into 3x chips if thesis intact
Tiny space kicker, keep with strict…

plan: Double SOXL from 6% to 10% — this is the aggressive chip bet; the 3x structure means a 5% SMH rally translates to roughly 15% on this sleeve, but a 5% SMH drop hits you roughly -15% on the same position, so a pre-set stop is non-negotiable.
Add to SMH from 13% to 16% to pair the leveraged chip exposure with a direct, non-decaying semiconductor position — combined chip complex becomes roughly 26% of book.
Add CRWD to 15% — it is already up +7.4% and is the only position in your current book showing clear positive momentum; this leans into what is working.

Open ARM at scanner entry 336.80-337.13 with hard stop at 336.18 — the R of 9.46 is the highest available in your data and the ACCUMULATE 7/12 score supports it; position at 5% keeps it sized but meaningful.

Open PANW at scanner entry 351.34-351.69 stop 350.62 — ACCUMULATE 9/12 is the strongest conviction signal in your dataset; paired with ARM this gives two quality AI/software longs alongside the chip complex.
Trim QQQ to 10% to fund the above adds without blowing through cash buffer — SPY at 30% keeps broad index coverage so cutting QQQ is the cleanest source of capital here.
aggressive.read: This plan meaningfully raises true leverage above the current 1.14x and pushes the semiconductor-plus-leveraged-ETF complex to roughly 26% of book, which could target 4%+ monthly return if chips and AI names continue rallying — but your 2-sigma bad-month drawdown likely grows well beyond the current -17.91%, and SOXL's daily rebalancing decay erodes value steadily in choppy or sideways markets regardless of direction.
